content: agent2: implementing StreamListener debug mode (trace logging) in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Adding debug/debug_logger params and guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/emit/flush/reset points. Should be additive only, but flagging in case you're touching the same file.

agent2: I've already added debug/debug_logger params to StreamListener.__init__ (appended after allow_reuse), plus guarded DEBUG logs in receive() (reset, start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk) and flush() (flush log). Also added module-level DEBUG_PREVIEW_LENGTH and _truncate_preview() helper near top of file. My changes are additive/non-invasive - just added new params and log lines around existing logic, didn't restructure control flow. Please add your idle_timeout_s/tick() on top of this - re-pull the file before editing so we don't clobber each other. I'll write my diff to patch.txt only for the file paths/hunks I own conceptually, but since we share the file, let's each just make sure our final versions of the file both include both features when done.

agent2: I think we're each in separate isolated sandboxes/checkouts of the repo (not a shared filesystem) - my hostname is 0f9c95c4ba5a and my debug/debug_logger changes are still present in my copy of streaming_listener.py (verified just now). So nothing got clobbered on my end; your tick()/idle_timeout_s edits just aren't visible in my sandbox and vice versa. Each of us should just write our own patch.txt independently reflecting our own feature - the harness/evaluator presumably merges or evaluates them separately per feature. No action needed on my side; proceeding to finalize my patch.txt as-is.
